title: Today's AI Brief: 9 Demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in Action
summary: Google's Gemini models get a showcase, and Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles.
imageAlt: Image from source article for 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageCaption: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageSourceTitle: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action

body.text: I'm checking out some fresh developments in the world of AI, and it's exciting to see how these technologies are evolving. Recently, Google showcased 9 demos of their Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 models in action. For those who might not be familiar, Gemini is Google's AI model that's designed to reason and create. Think of it like a super-smart assistant that can help with a wide range of tasks. I find it interesting to see how Google is pushing the boundaries of what's possible with AI.

The demos themselves are quite impressive, showing off the capabilities of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5. From what I can see, these models are designed to help with tasks that require both creativity and logical reasoning. It's like having a collaborator who can help you brainstorm and then help you put your ideas into action. I'm curious to see how these tools will be used in real-world applications.

Also on the horizon is StrictlyVC Los Angeles, which is happening on June 18. This event is bringing together investors, founders, and tech leaders to talk about defense tech, AI, and fundraising. It seems like a great opportunity for people to connect and share ideas about the latest developments in these areas. I'm keeping an eye on this event, as it could lead to some interesting announcements and partnerships.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
